title: triphysaria eriantha
intro: Triphysaria eriantha, also known as sky lupine, is a species of flowering plant in the family Scrophulariaceae. It is an annual plant native to the western United States and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to stimulate digestion, relieve respiratory issues, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system. Triphysaria eriantha can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is often used in folk medicine to treat a range of ailments, including bronchitis, coughs, and digestive disorders.
